ocr.text: Ethanol-Induced Face-Brain Dysmorphology
Figure 7. Unique facial dysmorphology induced by stage-specific ethanol exposure in the mouse corresponds to distinct clinical
phenotypes. Along with a control animal (A), representative examples of fetuses severely affected by ethanol exposure on GD7 (B) and GD8.5 (C)
are shown. The elongated upper lip with deficient philtrum of the GD7 exposed mouse mimics that seen in children with full-blown FAS [50]. The
‘‘fish-shaped’’ upper lip and bulbous nasal tip of the mouse exposed at GD8.5 resembles that of children with DiGeorge syndrome [37]. For the
animals shown, the face and brain can be visualized concurrently in 3D in Movie S1.
